name: United Explorer Card
effective_cost: Low if you check bags and fly United

verdict.text: Keep only if the free bag + 2 lounge passes clear the fee for you

earn_note: Chase markets '9x total miles' on United flights, but 6x of that is the base MileagePlus member earn you'd get on any United ticket regardless of card — the Explorer itself only adds 3x. Thin earning outside United/hotels/dining; this is a perks card, not an everyday-spend card.

points.note: United uses dynamic pricing with no published award chart, so value swings by route and date. No blackout dates and miles never expire. Cardholders get a baseline 10%+ discount on award tickets (15%+ once you hold Premier status).

transfer_partners.highlight: MileagePlus can't be transferred out to other loyalty programs — miles earned here are locked into United.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: You can transfer INTO MileagePlus from Chase Ultimate Rewards (1:1, via a Sapphire or Ink Preferred card) and from Marriott Bonvoy — useful for topping up an award a few thousand miles short.

credits.name: Free Checked Bag
credits.subtitle: 1st bag · you + 1 companion
credits.description: First checked bag free on United-operated flights for the primary cardholder and one companion on the same reservation.
tips: ~$35 each way — one round trip for two people covers most of the annual fee on its own.
You don't need to buy the ticket with this card, just have it in your wallet and be a Cardmember on the reservation.

credits.name: United Travel Credit
credits.subtitle: $100 · after $10k spend
credits.description: Statement credit toward United purchases after you spend $10,000 on the card within a calendar year.
tips: Only worth counting on if you're already spending $10k/yr on this card for other reasons.

credits.name: United Hotels Credit
credits.subtitle: $50 × 2 · prepaid stays
credits.description: Two separate $50 statement credits on prepaid hotel stays booked through United's hotel portal.
tips: Requires prepaying through United's portal, not a direct hotel booking — check the property list first.

credits.name: Rideshare Credit
credits.subtitle: $5/mo · enroll
credits.description: Monthly U.S. rideshare statement credit after enrollment.
tips: Small but nearly automatic if you already take rideshares — one ride a month captures it.

credits.name: Avis/Budget Credit
credits.subtitle: $25 × 2 rentals
credits.description: Statement credit on up to two Avis or Budget rentals per year.
tips: Only useful if you already rent from Avis/Budget specifically.

credits.name: JSX Flight Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $100/yr
credits.description: Statement credit on bookings with JSX, a semi-private hop-on jet service in limited U.S. markets.
tips: Irrelevant unless JSX actually flies a route you use — it's a small regional network.

credits.name: Instacart Credit
credits.subtitle: $10/mo + 3-mo Instacart+
credits.description: Up to $10/month in Instacart statement credits, plus a 3-month complimentary Instacart+ trial (then 25% off the annual rate).
tips: Monthly, no rollover — needs a recurring Instacart order to capture in full.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck / NEXUS Credit
credits.subtitle: $120 · every 4 years
credits.description: Statement credit for a Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS application fee, once every four years. Shown here amortized to an annual figure.
tips: Apply for Global Entry, not just TSA PreCheck — it includes TSA PreCheck and covers the same credit.

insurance.detail: Primary · up to $60,000 (secondary for NY residents)

protection_note: Solid on baggage and purchase protection, and the primary rental CDW is free and real ($60k, matches CSR's structure). The weak spot is trip cancellation/interruption — $1,500/person and $6,000/trip is thin next to CSR's $10,000/person, and the 12-hour trip-delay trigger is double CSR's 6-hour window.
rental_note: Primary coverage up to $60,000 (secondary only for New York residents) — decline the counter's CDW and pay with the card. Slightly lower cap than CSR's $75k but still free, real primary coverage.

status_perks.name: United Club one-time passes
status_perks.note: Two passes per year (one at account opening, one each anniversary) — enough for an occasional lounge visit, not real access. Club Infinite is the card for that.

status_perks.name: Premier Qualifying Points
status_perks.note: 1 PQP per $20 spent, capped at 1,000/yr — a slow crawl toward Premier Silver (5,000 PQP), not a real status-earning card.

services.name: Priority boarding
services.detail: Priority boarding on United-operated flights for the primary cardholder and companions on the reservation.

services.name: 25% back on inflight purchases
services.detail: Uncapped statement credit rebate on food, beverages, and Wi-Fi purchased onboard United-operated flights.

services.name: Award flight discount
services.detail: 10,000-mile discount on an award redemption after $20,000 in annual card spend.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

services.name: Visa Signature Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 concierge for reservations, tickets, and travel arrangements.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Free to add — no published annual fee for authorized users
10,000 bonus miles for adding one within the first 3 months (new accounts)
Earns MileagePlus miles (into the primary account)
Own United Club one-time passes or PQP allotment
Free checked bag only when traveling with the primary cardholder on the same reservation

additional_cards.note: Adding an authorized user is free and was worth 10,000 bonus miles during the current offer, but it doesn't add its own set of lounge passes or PQP — the free-bag perk only applies when the AU is actually traveling with the primary cardholder.

timeline.text: Fee $99 → $150 for existing cardholders; added United Hotels, rideshare, Avis/Budget, JSX, and Instacart credits alongside the existing free bag and Global Entry credit.
Chase and United announced a broad refresh across the whole United card lineup, moving from a single lounge-light benefit set toward a bundle of smaller category credits.
